ZMAT_DE                          OK — will create 
ZMAT_DOC                       Warning — namespace

If the simulation shows errors, do not proceed. Fix the root cause (namespace, missing dependency, locked object) before continuing. 

Confirm and assign transport request 

If the simulation is clean, confirm the prompt. The system may ask you to assign a new transport request in the target system. This is the TR that will carry the downported objects.

Assign workbench request — target system 

Request                     QAS K200456 (auto-created)
Description               Downport: DEV K900123 feature 
Target                        QAS (quality assurance)

UDO performs two parallel actions: SCWB copies code-only objects; UDO itself handles DDIC, documentation, message classes, and table contents. Both results land in the same new TR.

UDO report is generated and shipped

UDO generates an ABAP program called UDO_NOTE_<SAPNoteNumber> (BW: RS_UDO_NOTE_<X>). This report is stored in the deliverable package and published inside the corresponding SAP Note on SAP ONE Support Portal.

Generated UDO report — SE38 in target 

Program name                UDO_NOTE_2367512
Package                           Normal deliverable package
TR                                    QAS K200456
Published in note            SAP Note #2367512

Customer systems download and execute this report via SNOTE (Note Assistant). When executed, it creates and activates all DDIC objects and documentation in the customer landscape automatically.

Monitor and verify:-

After the copy completes, verify all objects are correctly ported in the target system. 

SE11                   —     check DDIC objects exist and are active in the target system
SE80 / SE38       —     verify ABAP programs and function groups are present
SE63 / F1 help   —     confirm documentation is readable in target language
SE91                   —     check message classes and message texts are in place
SE10                   —     review the new TR and release it through the landscape as normal
STMS                 —     import the TR into QAS and then PRD via normal transport route

For upporting (lower to higher release), the same TCode and flow apply — just swap which system is source and which is target, and run UDO from the higher-release target system.

Key tools in the UDO ecosystem:-

TCode 

Role in the process 

SM59 

Create/verify the RFC dialog connection between systems 

UDO 

Main transaction — compare, simulate, and copy objects 

SE10 

Release the new TR created by UDO in the target system 

STMS 

Transport the released TR through QAS → PRD 

SNOTE 

Customers apply the generated UDO_NOTE_X report from SAP Notes 

SE11/SE80/SE91 

Verify DDIC, programs, message classes after porting 

How it works technically:

The UDO tool compares the active version of an ABAP object in the remote system (the source that has the feature) with the version in the local system (where the feature is to be ported). It then collects DDIC changes and documentation changes and prepares a new ABAP program, usually called UDO_NOTE_X or in BW area RS_UDO_NOTE_X, where X is the SAP Note number. 

The execution flow in the target system:  

In the target system, start transaction UDO. Enter the transport request from the source system in the Transport Request(s) field. If you only want to transfer documentation and not development objects, select the Only Documentation Objects checkbox. You can use the glasses icon to look at the object in the target and the binoculars icon to look at it in the source. Select the rows containing the objects you want to copy and click the Copy Selected Objects icon — UDO then performs a test (simulation) run first and shows what the results would be.

Conclusion:

In this blog post, we explored the complete process of performing Upport and Downport in SAP using Transaction Code UDO. We discussed how developers can efficiently transfer corrections and developments across different system landscapes while maintaining code consistency and minimizing manual effort.

Using TCode UDO simplifies transport synchronization between systems and helps teams manage parallel development activities more effectively. Understanding the Upport and Downport process is especially important in large SAP projects where multiple releases and support packages are handled simultaneously.

By following the steps and best practices covered in this guide, SAP developers and support teams can improve transport management, reduce inconsistencies, and ensure smoother deployment across environments.

entry.title: Sapphire 2026 recap: Joule for Developers Agentic ABAP AI is generally available
entry.content:
TL;DR: What you learned on Sapphire 2026 is that in general SAP is evolving from standalone AI tools to an orchestration layer that automates multi-step workflows for business user and developers.
The ABAP platform team is very proud to be on the forefront of this and this was the major focus for all Sapphire ABAP AI sessions. 
In a nutshell we have announced the following key benefits for customers and partners of what will be made generally available in the first weeks of June:

 

  1. ABAP AI now supports Agentic orchestration in Eclipse and VS Code with out-of-the-box ABAP MCP Server lots of great MCP tools.
  2. ABAP AI is available for lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition >=2021.
  3. Simplified enablement of ABAP AI via SAP for Me and Central Business Configuration with a new commercial model based on AI Unit consumption (->details how to purchase and on-board / AI Units overview / Learning / AI calculator).
  4. Faster innovation with Side-by-Side consumption of SAP and 3rd party Agentic IDE, Orchestrators and LLMs (e.g. Microsoft, Amazon, IBM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral…)
  5. Agentic Custom Code Migration accelerates ECC to S/4HANA Transformation -> see the demo below or read the blog

 

Here is a demo of the first iteration of the agentic custom code migration with mass ready ATC AI quick fixes using Amazon Q in Eclipse (on Sapphire we showed also GitHub CoPilot as orchestrator):

If you want to understand deeper the context of the demo have a look into my Sapphire recap. Here I explain in more detail what it means for you as customer and developer and I also give a bit of outlook to what comes next:

More details can be found here:

  1. More videos on ABAP AI and custom code migration
  2. Sapphire 2026: GA announcement for agentic ABAP AI (Sonja Lienard)
  3. Sapphire 2026: Agentic Joule for Developers - your choice (Simona Marincei)
  4. Sapphire 2026: ABAP AI - Chapter 3: We go agentic! (Alexander Rother)
  5. Agentic Custom Code Migration for ECC to S/4HANA transformation (Olga Dolinskaja) / Custom Code Migration App
  6. How to purchase Agentic ABAP AI with AI Units
    • What are AI Units?  
    • SAP Business AI Commercial Model
    • AI commercial model learning 
    •  AI calculator
  7. VS Code: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace! 
  8. FAQ document for frequent questions
  9. Public Roadmap: SAP Help 
  10. How to extend ABAP Test Cockpit on SAP BTP for custom use cases
  11. Help & Guides
    1. What' New in ABAP GenAI
    2. SAP ABAP AI Discovery Center
    3. SAP BTP Discovery Center (Free Tier Services)
    4. How to Connect Claude Code to ABAP with Zero-Config MCP
    5. Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
    6. YouTube playlist for Joule for Developers (J4D)
    7. Joule for Developers Help (J4D)
    8. Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud and SAP Joule for developers (RAP120) 
    9. ADT Eclipse AI Capabilities Help
    10. ABAP AI SDK Help
    11. ABAP-1 Help

More high-level insights about Joule for Developers/ABAP AI and ABAP platform can be found here . Here you find more on ABAP- 1,

entry.title: ZSD_SO_FULFILL_MONITOR
entry.content:
&---------------------------------------------------------------------
*& Report ZSD_FULFILL_MONITOR
*& Developed By Shubham M
*& Date : 31.5.2026
&---------------------------------------------------------------------
*&
&---------------------------------------------------------------------
REPORT zsd_fulfill_monitor.

DATA:
  gv_vbeln TYPE vbak-vbeln,
  gv_erdat TYPE vbak-erdat,
  gv_kunnr TYPE vbak-kunnr,
  gv_matnr TYPE vbap-matnr.

SELECT-OPTIONS:
  s_vbeln FOR gv_vbeln,     "Sales Order Number
  s_erdat FOR gv_erdat,     "Sales Order Creation Date
  s_kunnr FOR gv_kunnr,     "Sold-to Party (Customer Number) VD03 -17100001 - 17411710
  s_matnr FOR gv_matnr.     "Material Number - 10 - 182

PARAMETERS:
  p_vkorg TYPE vbak-vkorg.  "Sales Organization e.g. Represents the selling unit within a company.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_sales,
         vbeln TYPE vbak-vbeln,
         erdat TYPE vbak-erdat,
         kunnr TYPE vbak-kunnr,
         vkorg TYPE vbak-vkorg,
       END OF ty_sales.

DATA: lt_sales TYPE TABLE OF ty_sales,
      ls_sales TYPE ty_sales.

      START-OF-SELECTION.

  SELECT vbeln,
         erdat,
         kunnr,
         vkorg
    FROM vbak
    INTO TABLE @LT_sales
    WHERE vbeln IN @s_vbeln
      AND erdat IN @s_erdat
      AND kunnr IN @s_kunnr
      AND vkorg = @p_vkorg.

  IF lt_sales IS NOT INITIAL.

    WRITE: / 'Sales Order',
             20 'Created On',
             35 'Customer',
             50 'Sales Org'.

    ULINE.

    LOOP AT lt_sales INTO ls_sales.

      WRITE: / ls_sales-vbeln,
               20 ls_sales-erdat,
               35 ls_sales-kunnr,
               50 ls_sales-vkorg.

    ENDLOOP.

  ELSE.

    WRITE: / 'No records found'.

  ENDIF.

entry.title: End to End Guide: Enabling SAP Joule in SAP S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad (PCE + OnPremise PoC)
entry.content:
1. Introduction

SAP Joule is SAP's generative AI, designed to help users accomplish tasks through natural language interaction within SAP applications. This guide will provide a step by step walkthrough to enable joule in SAP Fiori.

The guide is primarily written for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition (PCE).
PS: Also covers steps that differ for On-Premise S/4HANA (for a Proof-of-Concept environment).

2. Component Overview

Before starting, it is important to understand the key components involved and the role each plays.

Component

What It Is

Role in This Integration

SAP Joule

SAP's generative AI copilot. A cloud service on SAP BTP.

The AI assistant end users interact with. Loaded in FLP header as a Shell Plugin.

SAP BTP

SAP Business Technology Platform  the cloud foundation.

Hosts Joule, Work Zone, Identity Services, and destinations connecting to S/4HANA.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

SAP's digital workplace platform. Can serve as launchpad or content bridge.

Acts as the technical bridge between Joule and S/4HANA. Required in the Joule Formation.

SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)

SAP's Identity Authentication Service. Manages user identity and SSO.

Provides the SSO layer. All BTP services require IAS authentication.

SAP Cloud Connector (SCC)

An on-premise agent creating a secure tunnel from BTP to on-premise systems.

Enables Work Zone and Joule on BTP to securely reach S/4HANA.

SAP Fiori Launchpad (FLP)

The S/4HANA web UI hub where users launch Fiori apps.

The surface where the Joule icon appears via a Shell Plugin target mapping.

Formation

A BTP System Landscape construct grouping systems for a specific integration.

Tells Joule which Work Zone, IAS tenant, and S/4HANA belong together.

CDM3

Common Data Model v3 . SAP Fiori's content model (catalogs, tiles, groups).

Used to expose S/4HANA Fiori content to BTP so Work Zone can sync it.

3. Architecture Overview

The following describes the end-to-end integration flow:

  • End User opens S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad in browser
  • FLP authenticates via IAS (after SSO is configured)
  • FLP loads the Joule Shell Plugin from the Joule Web Client (sapdas.cloud.sap)
  • Joule on BTP connects to Work Zone via the Joule Formation
  • Work Zone reaches S/4HANA via BTP Destinations → Cloud Connector → ABAP Server(S/4 system)
  • Joule icon appears in FLP header. Users interact with Joule through natural language.

4. Prerequisites

Requirement

Details

SAP BTP Global Account

Admin access. Subaccount in the same region as your IAS tenant.

SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)

IAS tenant associated with your BTP Global Account.

SAP S/4HANA System

PCE or On-Premise (for PoC). FLP accessible via browser.

SAP Cloud Connector

Installed and running. Registered to your BTP subaccount.

S/4HANA Technical User

User with access to CDM3 service (/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3) and FLP admin roles.

BTP Entitlements

Joule (foundation), SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, SAP Cloud Identity Services.

Regional Alignment

BTP subaccount, IAS tenant, and Work Zone must be in the same region.

Note: PCE vs On-Premise: PCE uses token-based registration via Communication Arrangements. On-Premise uses Cloud Connector and manual destinations. Each step calls out differences.

5. Step-by-Step Guide

Step 1: Enable Cloud Foundry in BTP Subaccount

Why: Cloud Foundry is the runtime where BTP service instances (Work Zone, Joule) are deployed. Without it, subscriptions and the Joule booster cannot run.

  1. Open SAP BTP Cockpit → navigate to your target Subaccount → Overview.
  2. Click Enable Cloud Foundry. Accept defaults (Plan: standard). Click Create.
  3. Once enabled, click Create Space → name it e.g. 'dev'. Assign yourself Space Developer + Space Manager roles.

Validation: Subaccount Overview shows CF Enabled, Org Name, API Endpoint, and space 'dev' listed.

On-Premise PoC: No difference. This step is identical for both PCE and On-Premise.

 

Step 2: Add Entitlements

Why: Entitlements grant your subaccount the right to use specific BTP services. Without them, the services cannot be subscribed to or instantiated.

  1. Go to Global Account → Entitlements → Entity Assignments.
  2. Select your subaccount → Configure Entitlements → Add Service Plans.
  3. Add: Joule (also search as 'das-application') — plan: foundation
  4. Add: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition — plan: standard
  5. Add: SAP Cloud Identity Services — plan: connectivity
  6. Click Save.

Validation: All three services appear in Entitlements list with quota assigned.

Step 3: Establish Trust to IAS

Why: All BTP services (including Joule) authenticate via IAS. Establishing trust allows your subaccount to delegate authentication to IAS, enabling SSO across all connected services.

  1. Go to BTP Subaccount → Security → Trust Configuration.
  2. Click Establish Trust.
  3. Select your IAS tenant (e.g., yourtenantid.accounts.ondemand.com).
  4. Click Next through the wizard → Finish.

Validation: Trust Configuration shows your IAS tenant with status Active.

Step 4: Configure Trusted Domains in BTP

Why: BTP Trusted Domains control which browser origins can interact with your subaccount's services via iFrames and cross-origin calls. Without this, the Joule panel cannot be embedded in your FLP.

  1. Go to BTP Subaccount → Security → Settings → Trusted Domains.
  2. Click Add for each of the following origins (scheme + host + port if non-standard):
  • Your FLP origin: e.g. https://your-s4-host:44301
  • Your Joule Web Client: https://<tenant>.<region>.sapdas.cloud.sap
  • Your Work Zone site: https://<site-id>.launchpad.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com
  1. Click Save.

Note: Your Joule URL is found in System Landscape → Formations → your Joule formation → URL column of the Joule system entry.(You can maintain it later once Joule is activated via booster etc.)

Validation: All required origins appear in the Trusted Domains list.

Step 5: Configure Trusted Domains in IAS

Why: IAS maintains its own trusted domain allowlist. Cross-domain authentication requires IAS to trust the same origins as BTP.

  1. Open IAS Admin Console: https://<ias-tenant>.accounts.ondemand.com/admin
  2. Go to Applications & Resources → Tenant Settings → Customization → Trusted Domains.
  3. Click Add — enter hostname only (no scheme or port) for each host.
  4. Click Save.

Note: Format difference: BTP accepts full origins (https://host:44301). IAS accepts hostname only (host.example.com).

Validation: IAS shows 'Trusted Domains updated' confirmation.

Step 6: Expose SAP Fiori Launchpad Content (CDM3)

Why: Work Zone needs to know what Fiori apps and catalogs exist in S/4HANA. The CDM3 export makes this content discoverable so Work Zone can sync it.

Pre-requisite: Activate CDM3 ICF Service

  1. In S/4HANA, run transaction SICF.
  2. Navigate to /sap/bc/ui2/cdm3. If inactive, right-click → Activate Service.

Expose Content

  1. Run transaction /n/UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE.
  2. Select Version 1.
  3. Choose All roles (first-time setup) or select specific roles.
  4. Click Expose Content in the toolbar. Wait for completion.
  5. Click View Log to verify results.

Note: Warnings in the CDM3 log are normal. Red errors should be investigated but typically do not block the integration.

Validation: Log shows entities exposed (e.g., '14,663 out of 14,671 entities exposed'). Click View Exposed Content to confirm.

Step 7: Set Up SAP Cloud Connector

Why: Cloud Connector creates a secure outbound-only tunnel from your on-premise/PCE network to BTP. Work Zone and Joule use this tunnel to reach S/4HANA without inbound firewall rules.

Please setup cloud connector first.

7.1 Register BTP Subaccount

  1. Open SCC Admin UI (e.g. https://localhost:8443).
  2. Add your BTP subaccount using Download Authentication Data from BTP → Connectivity → Cloud Connectors.
  3. Confirm status shows Connected (green).

7.2 Create Back-End Mapping

  1. Go to Cloud to On-Premises → + Add:
  • Protocol: HTTPS (preferred) or HTTP (PoC)
  • Internal Host/Port: e.g. S/4HANA internal hostname + HTTPS port 44301 or HTTP port 8001
  • Virtual Host: a clean alias (e.g., s4hana-virtual)
  • Back-End Type: ABAP System
  1. Add Resources: /sap/bc/ui2/cdm3, /sap/bc/ui2/flp, /sap/bc/ui5_ui5 — all with 'Path and All Sub-Paths'.

Validation: Mapping shows green 'Reachable' status. BTP Cockpit → Connectivity → Cloud Connectors shows 'Available'.

On-Premise PoC: If HTTPS port 44301 is blocked between SCC and ABAP server, use HTTP port 8001 for PoC. In SICF, navigate to /sap/bc/ui2/cdm3 → Logon Data tab → check 'Use all logon procedures' to allow Basic Authentication.

Step 8: Activate Subscriptions and Instances

Why: Subscriptions activate the actual services in your subaccount. Without subscribing to Work Zone and Joule, neither the Joule icon nor the content bridge can function.

  1. Go to BTP Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions → Create.
  2. Subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition — plan: standard.
  3. Subscribe to Joule (das-application) — plan: foundation.
  4. For Work Zone: also create a Service Instance (name: BuildZone) and a Service Key.
  5. Launch Work Zone from Instances & Subscriptions at least once — this registers it in System Landscape.

Note: The Joule Booster (next step) looks for the Work Zone subscription in its dropdown. It only appears after you launch Work Zone at least once.

Validation: Subscriptions show 'Subscribed'. Service Key created. System Landscape → Systems shows Work Zone auto-registered.

Step 9: Configure the Joule Formation

Why: A Formation is a logical grouping that tells Joule which Work Zone, IAS tenant, and S/4HANA belong together. Without a formation, Joule has no context about which systems to connect to.

Option A — Run the Joule Booster (Recommended for PCE)

  1. Go to Global Account → Boosters → search 'Setting Up Joule' → click Start.
  2. Wizard: Select subaccount and CF space (dev).
  3. Step 3 — Select Integrations: choose SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.
  4. Step 4 — Select Capabilities: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition + SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.
  5. Step 5 — Select your Work Zone system and S/4HANA system.
  6. Click Execute and wait for completion.

Option B — Add Systems to Existing Formation

  1. Go to Global Account → System Landscape → Formations.
  2. Find the formation of type 'Integration with Joule' with status Ready.
  3. Click Include Systems → add your Work Zone instance and S/4HANA system.
  4. Confirm integration options (Enable Capability Deployment, Enable Joule Icon).

Validation: Formation shows status Ready (green). Includes IAS, Work Zone, and Joule systems. Note the Joule URL — needed for Trusted Domains and shell plugin.

On-Premise PoC: On-Premise systems cannot complete Communication Arrangement token registration.

Recommended approach:

(1) Run the booster selecting only Work Zone as the integration (not S/4HANA).
(2) Register S/4HANA manually in System Landscape → Systems → Add → Type: SAP S/4HANA Cloud. (Not needed for POC)

Step 10: Configure BTP Destinations

Why: BTP Destinations define how Work Zone and Joule connect to S/4HANA. Without correct destinations, Work Zone cannot read content and Joule cannot reach the backend.

Create the following destination (Design-Time and Runtime can be the same for PoC):

Property

Value

Name

e.g, jouletc  (lowercase, no underscores)

Type

HTTP

URL

http://<scc-virtual-host>:<port>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User / Password

S/4HANA technical user with CDM3 access

sap-client

Your S/4HANA client (e.g., 100)

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

WebIDEEnabled

true

WebIDEUsage

odata_abap

 

Critical: Work Zone Destination Naming: Work Zone only accepts destination names that are lowercase with no underscores. Names like MCF_TC will cause an error. Use names like jouletc or s4hana.

Critical: URL Path: The destination URL must include the CDM3 path: /sap/bc/ui2/cdm3. Without this, Work Zone receives an HTML login page instead of JSON, resulting in an authorization error.

Validation: Check Connection returns HTTP 200. e.g. SWZ_JOULE destination is auto-created and visible in the destinations list.

 

Step 11: Configure Work Zone Channel Manager

Why: The Channel Manager connects Work Zone to S/4HANA as a Content Provider. This sync imports S/4HANA's Fiori catalogs and makes the app landscape available to Joule.

  1. Launch SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition from Instances & Subscriptions.
  2. Go to Channel Manager → + New.
  3. Fill in:
  • Title: your system name (e.g., S4H_UIB)
  • Design-Time Destination: your lowercase destination (e.g., jouletc)
  • Runtime Destination: same
  • Content Scope: Roles and related content
  • Automatically add all content items: ON
  1. Click Save, then click the sync icon.

Wait 5-15 minutes for first sync

On-Premise PoC: The channel may show 'Updated' with a 'Connectivity Error' in the Report. This error is for real-time change notification callbacks, which on-premise systems do not support. The content sync itself succeeds. Re-sync manually when S/4HANA content changes.

Validation: Channel shows Updated status. Content Manager → Explore confirms S/4HANA catalogs are visible.

Step 12: Configure Joule Shell Plugin in S/4HANA FLP

Why: The Joule icon in FLP is delivered via a SAPUI5 Shell Plugin configured as a Target Mapping in an FLP catalog. Without this step, users will not see the Joule icon regardless of BTP configuration.

12.1 Create Technical Catalog

  1. In S/4HANA, run transaction /n/UI2/FLPD_CUST (Launchpad Content Manager: Cross-Client).
  2. In the Catalogs tab, create a new catalog: ID = Z_JOULE_TECH, Title = 'Joule Technical Catalog'. Package: $TMP (PoC).

12.2 Create Shell Plugin Target Mapping

  1. Open catalog Z_JOULE_TECH in the Launchpad Designer (opens in browser).
  2. Click Target Map... tab → Create Target Mapping.
  3. Fill in:
  • Semantic Object: Shell
  • Action: plugin  (type manually — not in dropdown)
  • Application Type: SAPUI5 Fiori App
  • URL: https://<joule-tenant>.<region>.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4  (NO trailing slash)
  • Title: Joule
  • ID: sap.das.webclientplugin.s4
  • Save.

Critical: No Trailing Slash: The Joule Web Client URL must NOT end with a slash. Use .../webclient/s4 not .../webclient/s4/. A trailing slash causes 'File not found' and the icon will not appear.

Note: How to find your Joule URL: Go to System Landscape → Formations → your Joule formation. The Joule system shows a URL like https://<tenant>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap. Append /resources/public/webclient/s4 to this base URL.

12.3 Create Role and Assign Catalog

  1. Run transaction PFCG → create single role Z_JOULE_S4HANA.
  2. Menu tab → click Launchpad Catalog → assign Z_JOULE_TECH (Local Front-End Server).
  3. Authorizations tab → Change Authorization Data → Do not select templates → Generate → Save.
  4. User tab → assign role to target users → User Comparison → Full Comparison → OK.

Validation: Log in to FLP. The Joule icon (sparkle/diamond) appears in the FLP header. Clicking it opens the Joule panel.

Step 13: Configure IAS SSO for S/4HANA FLP (Recommended)

Why: Joule is secured by IAS. If FLP uses ABAP local login, users must log in again when Joule opens. Configuring IAS SSO for FLP gives a seamless single login experience.

13.1 Download IAS Metadata

  1. Open IAS Admin Console → Applications & Resources → Tenant Settings → SAML 2.0 Configuration.
  2. Click Download Metadata File. Save the XML.

13.2 Configure SAML2 in S/4HANA

  1. Run transaction SAML2 in S/4HANA.
  2. Enable SAML 2.0 and create a Local Provider Name.
  3. Trusted Providers tab → Add → Upload Metadata File → upload IAS metadata.
  4. Set NameID format (Login Name recommended — maps to ABAP user ID).
  5. Download S/4HANA SP metadata.

13.3 Register S/4HANA as Application in IAS

  1. IAS Admin Console → Applications → + Add.
  2. Upload S/4HANA SP metadata under Trust → SAML 2.0 Configuration.
  3. Set Subject Name Identifier to match your ABAP user attribute (Login Name).
  4. Save.

Validation: Opening FLP URL redirects to IAS login. After login, FLP opens without ABAP credential prompts. Joule opens without a separate login.

6. Troubleshooting

Symptom

Likely Cause

Fix

Joule icon not visible in FLP

Shell plugin not loaded or role not assigned

Verify role Z_JOULE_S4HANA is assigned. Hard-reload FLP (Ctrl+Shift+R). Check browser console for plugin errors.

'File not found' when Joule loads

Trailing slash in plugin URL

Remove trailing slash: use .../webclient/s4 not .../webclient/s4/.

'Refused to frame/connect' error

Missing Trusted Domain entry

Add the exact blocked origin (shown in browser console) to BTP and IAS Trusted Domains.

Work Zone Channel: Connectivity Error

Real-time callbacks not supported (on-premise)

If status shows 'Updated' with Connectivity Error — content synced. Re-sync manually as needed.

Work Zone 'authorization error' on fetch

Destination URL missing CDM3 path

Ensure destination URL is http://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3.

Booster fails: 'System already part of formation'

IAS can only be in one Joule formation

Find the existing Joule formation and add your systems to it instead.

SCC mapping 'Not Reachable'

Firewall blocking the port

Ask network team to open the port between SCC and ABAP server. Try internal IP instead of hostname.

Joule opens but prompts for login

IAS SSO not configured for FLP

Complete Step 13 — configure SAML2 trust between S/4HANA and IAS.

Work Zone destination name error

Name has uppercase or underscores

Rename destination to lowercase with no underscores (e.g., jouletc).

Wrong Joule URL after S/4HANA 2025 upgrade

DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN set to pilot URL

Update /UI2/FLP_SYS_CONF (SM30) with your tenant URL, or remove the parameter.

7. Summary — What We Achieved

  • Joule provisioned on SAP BTP and linked to IAS and Work Zone via a Formation
  • SAP Cloud Connector established as the secure bridge between BTP and S/4HANA
  • Work Zone Channel Manager syncing S/4HANA Fiori catalogs to BTP
  • BTP Destinations correctly routing Work Zone to S/4HANA via Cloud Connector
  • Joule Shell Plugin configured in S/4HANA FLP via a Technical Catalog and PFCG role
  • Joule icon visible and functional in the S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad

entry.title: 在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中通过自定义逻辑在创建外向交货单时实现库存数量不足提醒
entry.content:
问题背景

有客户提出需求，由于业务管理要求,希望在创建外向交货单时能够自动检查交货单行项目物料的库存数量是否充足。具体来说，当外向交货单创建保存时，需要检查交货单对应的销售订单行项目物料数量是否大于物料库存数量，如果库存数量不足，则增加一个 Warning 提示该物料库存数量不足。同时，客户希望在使用 API 创建外向交货单时，也能看到对应的 Message。

解决方案

尝试通过自定义逻辑,使用两个 BAdI 的组合方案来满足需求。

分析步骤

1. 确认适用的 BAdI 增强点

首先,我们需要找到合适的 BAdI 增强点来实现库存检查功能。经过分析,发现增强点 LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK 可以在交货单保存之前执行最终检查。因此,可以通过这个 BAdI 实现在前台 APP 创建外向交货单时，如果库存数量不足，返回 Warning 消息提醒。

2. 实现前台 APP 的 Warning 提醒

在 BAdI LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK 中实现基本的库存检查逻辑：

    CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant    TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg    TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg    TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart    TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

    DATA:
    lv_so_qty    TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

    IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
      LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item) .
      IF ls_item-plant = lc_plant.
      " 读取销售订单行项目的订单数量
      SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
      " 读取物料的库存数量
      SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
      FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
      INTO @LV_stock.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE. " No SO item found – skip this delivery item
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
        message-messagetype = 'W'. " saving of the delivery document will be warned
        message-messagetext = |Warning: 该物料库存数量不足|.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

3. 发现 API 场景的局限性

但是这里的 Warning Message 只能在前台 APP 显示，API 创建的情况下无法看到 Message。因此需要通过替代方案实现。

4. 实现 API 场景的消息提示

为了让 API 创建场景也能看到提示信息，我们采用以下方案：

方案说明：

- 创建一个自定义字段，这个字段可以在 API Response 里看到。我们创建了一个自定义字段
- 使用 BAdI LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE，这个 BAdI 可以修改表头和行项目级别的标准字段和自定义字段
- 该 BAdI 将在交货单保存到数据库之前被调用

实现代码：

IF DOCUMENTPROCESSINGMODE = 'CREATE'.
CONSTANTS:
  lc_plant  TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
  lc_vkorg  TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
  lc_vtweg  TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
  lc_spart  TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

DATA:
  lv_so_qty TYPE menge_d,
  lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

" -------------------------------------------------------
" Step 1: 必须先将输入完整复制到输出（Cloud BAdI 标准做法）
" -------------------------------------------------------
move-corresponding delivery_document_in to delivery_document_out.
delivery_document_items_out = delivery_document_items_in.

" -------------------------------------------------------
" Step 2: 表头组织数据过滤
" 仅对 Sales Org 1310 / DC 20 / Division 00 生效
" -------------------------------------------------------
*CHECK delivery_document_in-salesorganization = lc_vkorg.

" -------------------------------------------------------
" Step 3: 遍历交货单行项目
" -------------------------------------------------------
LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item).


  " 跳过无参考销售订单的行项目
*  CHECK ls_item-referencesddocument IS NOT INITIAL.
*  CHECK ls_item-referencesddocumentitem IS NOT INITIAL.

  " 读取销售订单行项目的订单数量
  SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
    FROM i_salesorderitem
    WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
      AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
    INTO @LV_so_qty.
  " 读取物料的库存数量
  SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
    FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
    WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
    INTO @LV_stock.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    CONTINUE. " 未找到对应销售订单行项目，跳过
  ENDIF.

  " 比较库存数量与销售订单数量
  IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
    " 更新自定义字段
    DELIVERY_DOCUMENT_OUT-yy1_message_dlh = 'Warning: 该物料库存数量不足。'.
  ENDIF.

ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

5. 使用到的 CDS View

在实现过程中,我们使用了以下 CDS View 来获取所需的数据:

- I_SalesOrderItem: 用于获取销售订单行项目的订单数量,通过销售订单号和行项目号进行查询
- I_StockQuantityCurrentValue_2: 用于获取物料的当前库存数量,提供了物料在不同存储地点的实时库存信息,非常适合用于库存检查场景

6. 测试验证

测试后，上述代码可以满足需求:

- 在前台 APP 创建外向交货单时，如果库存不足，会显示 Warning 消息

 

- 通过 API 创建外向交货单时，自定义字段的值被更改，可以在 Response 的自定义字段中看到库存不足的提示信息

 

结语

通过阅读本文以及之前写过的几篇相似场景的 Blog，希望您已知晓在创建外向交货单时，如何通过自定义逻辑来实现库存数量检查并在不同场景下返回相应的提示消息。

关注以下链接可以看到更多有关 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 销售相关的问题和博文：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales - SAP Community

如果您对本文有任何的建议和想法,欢迎在评论区提出,期待与您一起交流。也欢迎您关注我的个人账号，期待我们在下一篇博文再见！

entry.title: Deploy a SAP Fiori Elements App from BAS to S/4HANA Cloud and Register It as a Launchpad Tile
entry.content:
Most tutorials about deploying Fiori apps from Business Application Studio (BAS) stop at 'Deployment Successful.' But deployment is only the midpoint. For a user to actually find and launch your app in the SAP Fiori Launchpad, you need to wire up four more things: a crossNavigation inbound in the manifest, an IAM App, a Business Catalog, and a Launchpad Space. Get any one of these wrong and you hit silent failures — the app exists in the system but never shows as a tile.

Prerequisites

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) subaccount with SAP Business Application Studio subscription
  • S/4HANA Cloud system with an active OData V4 service
  • A BTP Destination configured for your S/4HANA system
  • Eclipse ADT connected to your S/4HANA system

Architecture Overview

OData V4 Service (S/4HANA ABAP)
        │
        ▼
Fiori Elements List Report App  ◄── Generated in SAP BAS
        │
   npm run deploy  (deploy-to-abap, ui5-deploy.yaml, gCTS transport)
        │
        ▼
BSP Application + LADI /NAMESPACE/MYAPP_UI5R  (auto-created on deploy)
        │
        ▼
IAM App (External App type)  ◄── Created in ADT, Published Locally
        │
        ▼
Business Catalog  ◄── Created in ADT, Published Locally
        │
        ▼
Business Role  ◄── Assigned in Maintain Business Roles
        │
        ▼
Launchpad Space / Page  ◄── Tile visible to user

 

 

Step 1 — Open Business Application Studio via BTP Cockpit

BAS is accessed through the SAP BTP Cockpit — not through the S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad. Open BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions.

Find SAP Business Application Studio → click Go to Application. You land in the SAP Build Lobby.

Step 2 — Create the Project in SAP Build

In the SAP Build Lobby, click Create (top right) to launch the Create Project wizard.

Wizard Step 1 — Objective: Application

Wizard Step 2 — Category: Full-Stack

Wizard Step 3 — Type: Full-Stack Node.JS

Wizard Step 4 — Name and Dev Space

Provide a project name (e.g. myapp-audit-log) and create or select a Full-Stack dev space.

Click Review then Create. SAP Build provisions the dev space and scaffolds the project.

Step 3 — Generate the Fiori App

With the project open in BAS, press Ctrl+Shift+P and run: Fiori: Open Application Generator

Template Selection — List Report Page

Data Source — Connect to a System

  • Data Source: Connect to a System
  • System: Your S/4HANA destination (e.g. YOUR_SYSTEM (S4HC))

Once connected, choose your OData V4 service.

Entity Selection

  • Main Entity: Root CDS entity exposed by the service
  • Automatically add table columns: Yes
  • Table Type: Responsive

Click Finish. BAS generates and opens the project.

Step 4 — Add the crossNavigation Inbound to manifest.json

⚠️  This is where most guides go wrong by omission.

By default the Fiori generator creates an application-type app with no crossNavigation block. If you deploy as-is, the BAS terminal shows:

warn  No LADI was created as no inbound is defined in manifest.json
warn  App descriptor of type 'application' does not define a launchable inbound

Without a Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI), you cannot create an IAM App that references your app, and therefore cannot add a tile to a Business Catalog.

Fix: Open webapp/manifest.json and add a crossNavigation block inside sap.app:

"sap.app": {
  "id": "com.mycompany.myauditlog",
  "type": "application",
  "crossNavigation": {
    "inbounds": {
      "MyAuditLog-display": {
        "semanticObject": "MyAuditLog",
        "action": "display",
        "title": "{{flpTitle}}",
        "subTitle": "{{flpSubtitle}}",
        "signature": {
          "parameters": {},
          "additionalParameters": "allowed"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Also add the keys to webapp/i18n/i18n.properties:

flpTitle=My Audit Log App
flpSubtitle=View sales order change history

 

📝  Tip on additionalParameters: Setting it to "" produces a harmless warning. Use the explicit string "allowed" or "ignored" to avoid it.

 

 

Step 5 — Create a gCTS Transport Request in ADT

The deploy-to-abap task requires a gCTS transport — a Workbench Request with target 1GT. A normal transport fails with:

Request does not have transport target 1GT

In ADT: Transport Organizer → right-click your user node → Create → Transport Request:

  • Type: Workbench Request
  • Target: 1GT

Note the transport number (e.g. ABC9xxxxx) — you will plug it into ui5-deploy.yaml.

Step 6 — Configure ui5-deploy.yaml

Create ui5-deploy.yaml in the project root:

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: com.mycompany.myauditlog
type: application
builder:
  resources:
    excludes:
      - /test/**
      - /localService/**
  customTasks:
    - name: deploy-to-abap
      afterTask: generateCachebusterInfo
      configuration:
        target:
          destination: YOUR_SYSTEM
          authenticationType: reentranceTicket
        app:
          name: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG
          description: My Audit Log App
          package: /MYNAMESPACE/MY_PACKAGE
          transport: ABCK9xxxxx
        exclude:
          - /test/
          - /localService/

⚠️  app.name must use your registered ABAP namespace prefix (e.g. /MYNAMESPACE/). Using a Z* name in a namespaced package causes: Customer object WAPA cannot be assigned to package

Step 7 — Build and Deploy

Open a terminal in the project root and run:

npm install
npm run build
npm run deploy

A successful deployment shows:

Inbound MyAuditLog-display was converted to LADI /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R
Launchpad App Descriptor Item /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R was created
SAPUI5 Application has been uploaded and registered successfully
Deployment Successful.

📝  Note the LADI ID (/MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R) — you need it for the IAM App in the next step.

Step 8 — Create the IAM App in ADT

In ADT: right-click your package → New → Other ABAP Repository Object → search for IAM App.

Fill in:

  • Name: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_APP
  • Description: My Audit Log Fiori App
  • App Type: External App  ← critical (see gotcha below)
  • Fiori Launchpad App Descr Item ID: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R

Select your transport request and click Finish.

Click Publish Locally.

⚠️  Do NOT select 'Fiori App' or 'UI Adaptation App' as the type. The UI5R descriptor created by deploy-to-abap is only compatible with External App. Wrong type gives:
    UI5R can only be used in Adaptation UI Apps

📝  Publishing auto-generates _UI5A and _UI5_EXT runtime artifacts. Always reference the IAM App by its logical name /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_APP, not the runtime objects. 

Step 9 — Create and Publish the Business Catalog

In ADT: right-click your package → New → Other ABAP Repository Object → Business Catalog.

Name it (e.g. /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_BC), select your transport, click Finish.

In the Apps tab, click Add and search for your logical IAM App name.

Save, then click Publish Locally.

⚠️  The catalog MUST be Published before it appears in:
  • The Maintain Business Roles app's Business Catalog picker
  • The Select Catalogs dialog in the Launchpad page editor
If you can see a colleague's catalog but not yours, come back and verify the Publication State.

Step 10 — Assign the Catalog to a Business Role

In the Fiori Launchpad, open the Maintain Business Roles app:

  • Open your role → Edit
  • Business Catalogs tab → Add → search for /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_BC → OK
  • Set Access Category to Unrestricted
  • Save

⚠️  The Select Catalogs dialog in the page editor (next step) only lists catalogs already assigned to the role that owns the space. Skip this step and your catalog won't appear in the dialog even if it's published. 

Step 11 — Add the Tile to a Launchpad Space

In Maintain Business Roles → your role → Launchpad Spaces tab:

  1. Click Add → Create New Space

  1. Open the space/page → Edit → click Select Catalogs

  1. Tick your catalog → Select
  2. On the Manually Selected tab, drag the tile into the page
  3. Add a Section Title, then Save

Step 12 — Access the App

Hard-refresh the Fiori Launchpad (Ctrl+Shift+R). Navigate to your space.

Click the tile. The List Report loads. Press Go.

Troubleshooting Cheat Sheet

Symptom

Root Cause

Fix

warn No LADI was created

No crossNavigation.inbounds in manifest.json

Add the crossNavigation block before deploying

Request does not have transport target 1GT

Wrong transport type

Create a gCTS transport (target 1GT) in ADT

Customer object WAPA cannot be assigned to package

Z* app name in a namespaced package

Use namespaced app name: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAPP

UI5R can only be used in Adaptation UI Apps

Wrong IAM App type

Create IAM App with type External App

My catalog missing from Select Catalogs

Not published or not assigned to the role

Publish in ADT + assign to Business Role + reload

403 Forbidden on BSP URL

UCON blocks direct access

Access via the Launchpad tile, not the direct URL

 

Summary

The key insight this guide captures is that deployment and launchpad registration are separate concerns that must be completed correctly and in order:

  1. crossNavigation inbound → enables LADI creation on deploy
  2. gCTS transport → enables deploy-to-abap
  3. IAM App (External App) → links the LADI to the authorization layer
  4. Business Catalog (Published) → groups the app for role assignment
  5. Business Role (catalog assigned) → makes the catalog visible in the page editor
  6. Launchpad page (tile dragged in) → makes the tile visible to the user

 Tested on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Build / BAS (2024/2025 release), @SAP-ux/ui5-deploy task via specVersion: '4.0' ui5-deploy.yaml.

entry.title: How to Connect Claude Code to ABAP with Zero-Config MCP
entry.content:
Before We Start - What This Post Deliberately Skips
- What is Claude Code, and what are the available models?
- What is MCP (Model Context Protocol) and how it works conceptually?
- What is vibe coding and why AI-assisted development matters?
- What are the benefits of using AI in SAP development?
- What development platforms are available (Web, Desktop, CLI)?
If you need more details, you can find these topics in sap community portal. Read that, then come back here.

What This Post Covers - and Why It Matters?

This post walks you through connecting Claude Code to a live SAP system using the ABAP Remote Filesystem extension's native MCP server - no external Node.js setup, no manual JSON editing, just a single toggle. 

What this changes for SAP development:

  • No more hand-typed routine code. TYPE declarations, SELECT statements, ALV setup - written directly into the SAP object from a single prompt.
  • No more AI guesswork. Without a live connection, AI tools hallucinate tables and function modules that don't exist. With MCP, every suggestion is grounded in your actual system.
  • No more setup overhead. What used to require a custom Node.js project now takes one checkbox and one command - open to any developer with VS Code.
  • Clean Core by default. Claude works through the same ADT API and released objects that Eclipse uses, so generated code fits your governance model instead of fighting it.

This is the point where AI-assisted ABAP stops being a chat-window experiment and starts being a real part of your IDE.

 
Goal: I will cover the entire topic in 4 parts:
|Part 1| Installing Claude Code on Windows and setting up the VS Code extension |
|Part 2| Connecting VS Code to your live SAP system via the ABAP FS extension |
|Part 3| Enabling the native MCP server with a single toggle - no Node.js setup, no JSON editing |
|Part 4| A live demo: one plain-English prompt creates a real, running ABAP report on SAP |
If you're here, you already know why this matters. Let's get to the actual steps.
 
Pre-requisite: This guide assumes you already have an active Claude subscription (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise). If not, sign up at claude.ai before proceeding
 
Part 1 - The Setup: Getting Claude Code Running on Windows
Step 1.1 - Install Claude Code CLI
Open PowerShell (the built-in Windows command-line shell) and run:

 

This downloads Anthropic's official installer and executes it in one step. 'irm' fetches the script, 'iex' runs it.
> ⚠️ Common Error - Git Bash Not Found
> Claude Code needs a bash shell to run on Windows. If you see this error, install Git for Windows from [git-scm.com/downloads/win](https://git-scm.com/downloads/win). During setup, select: Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software. Then re-run the install command above.
Step 1.2 — Verify the Installation

 

Close PowerShell and open a fresh window. Run:
You should see a version number like 'claude 1.x.x'. If PowerShell says claude is not recognized, the PATH wasn't set automatically. Fix it manually:

 

1. Start → search Environment Variables in system settings
2. Edit the environment variables for your account
3. Select Path → Edit → New → paste ( in this case path can be taken from powershell commnad prompt)
 for eg: 

 

4. Close and reopen PowerShell, then re-run claude --version.

> Run claude doctor - this command checks your PATH, authentication, Node.js version, and config in one shot. Always the first step when something isn't working.
Step 1.3 - Launch Claude Code and the VS Code Extension
Navigate to your project folder and launch Claude Code.
The first launch opens your browser to authenticate with your Anthropic account (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise all work). 
For everyday ABAP work, the VS Code extension is where you'll spend most of your time.
Download and Install VS code from code.visualstudio.com for windows if you don't already have it. Once it is installed.Open VS Code, press 'Ctrl+Shift+X', search for Claude Code (published by Anthropic), and install it. Once installed, the Claude icon appears in the Activity Bar and connects automatically to your account.

 

The extension gives you a persistent chat panel inside VS Code - ask Claude about the file you have open, highlight any ABAP block and ask it to explain or refactor, and switch between ABAP and CAP projects in the same session. No terminal switching required.
> Note on Software Center installs: If your organization restricts installations, Claude Code for VS Code is typically available via your company Software Center / Company Portal.
 
Part 2 - Connecting VS Code to Your SAP System via ABAP FS
This is where the magic begins. The ABAP Remote Filesystem (ABAP FS)extension - built by Marcello Urbani - connects VS Code to your SAP system using the same ADT REST API that Eclipse uses. Once connected, your ABAP objects (programs, classes, CDS views, behavior definitions) appear as files in VS Code. Claude Code can then read, write, and review them like any local code file.
Pre-requisites Before You Connect
You'll need these four things from your SAP system before starting:
| Hostname or IP | The address of your SAP server (hostname preferred - IP causes SSL issues explained below) |
| ADT Service active | Go to transaction SICF → '/sap/bc/adt' must be activated |
| Client number| e.g. '100' - visible in the SAP logon screen |
|Username & Password| Your regular SAP development user |
| Port | HTTPS: '44300' (typical) | HTTP: '8000' for instance 00, '8001' for instance 01 |
 
Step 2.1 - Install and Configure ABAP FS
1. In VS Code Extensions ('Ctrl+Shift+X'), search ABAP remote filesystem by Marcello Urbani → Install.
2. Press 'Ctrl+Shift+P' → type ABAP FS: Connection Manager → open it.
3. Add a new connection and fill in the fields:
   - Name: anything you like (e.g. DEV or SANDBOX)
   - Server URL:'https://<hostname>:44300'
   - ⚠️ Do NOT add '/sap/bc/adt' to the URL - the extension adds this automatically. If you include it, the path doubles and the connection fails.
   - Client, Username, Language: 'EN'
4. Save.
5. Press 'Ctrl+Shift+P' → ABAP FS: Connect to an SAP system→ select your connection → enter password.
 

 

> ⚠️ SSL Certificate Error (very common on internal systems)
> If you see IP does not match certificate's altnames - this is because your SAP system's SSL certificate was issued with a hostname, not the IP. The fix: Get the hostname (e.g. 'https://sapdev.company.com:44300') and use that instead of the IP. 
> ⚠️ADT Service Not Found
> If you see Resource /sap/bc/adt does not exist - the ADT service isn't activated. Run tcode SICF → navigate to default_host → 'sap/bc/adt' → right-click → Activate Service.
 
Step 2.2 - Navigating ABAP Objects
ABAP FS gives you two ways to find objects. Object Search lets you find anything by name instantly. If you prefer a tree view like Eclipse ADT, enable SAP GUI Integration in your connection settings - this gives you a full package tree (Dictionary, Source Code Library, Transactions) and even embeds SAP GUI for HTML directly inside VS Code, so you can run transactions without leaving the editor.
1. Click OBJECT SEARCH in the left sidebar
2. Type the object or package name (e.g. 'ZCL_MY_CLASS' or 'Z_MY_PACKAGE')
3. Double-click any result to open it as a text file in VS Code
4. To edit: right-click  → edit → save (changes push back to SAP automatically)
Once an object is open in VS Code, open the integrated terminal (Ctrl+) and run claude. Some prompts that immediately pay off:

 

Part 3 -The Game Changer: Enabling the Native MCP Server

 

This is the step that changes everything - and the reason this post exists.
Why ABAP FS Alone Isn't Enough?
With only ABAP FS connected, Claude can see only the one file you currently have open. Ask it to create a new class and it might invent table names, function modules, or BAdI definitions that don't exist on your system. It's helpful, but it's guessing.
          ABAP FS Only                                         ABAP FS + MCP
Claude sees what we search Manually Claude autonomously searches any object on the system
Hallucinates objects it hasn't seen Grounded in live SAP system data
You copy-paste code manually Claude reads, writes, activates autonomously
No transport awareness Creates & assigns transports via ADT

 

The MCP (Model Context Protocol) server gives Claude Code a live connection to your SAP system's ADT API - the same backend Eclipse uses. Claude stops guessing and starts knowing.
Step 3.1 - Enable the MCP Server (One Toggle)
This is where the zero-config part comes in. You don't need to install anything extra, clone a Node.js repo, or edit JSON files manually.
Press 'Ctrl+Shift+P' → search ABAP FS MCP → select it → enable Autostart.
That's it. The ABAP FS extension spins up a local MCP server on your machine (default port 4847). You'll see a VS Code notification confirming it's running:
> ABAP MCP Server running on port 4847. External AI clients can connect to http://localhost:4847/mcp

Step 3.2 - Register the MCP Server with Claude Code
Now tell Claude Code where to find the ABAP tools. Run this in the VS Code integrated terminal:
This registers the local MCP server with Claude Code. The configuration is saved to your '.claude.json' file and persists across sessions - you only need to do this once.
Step 3.3 - Verify the Connection

 

open the terminal and run this command once the MCP server is added 
You should see:
Claude Code will confirm when the server is registered:
> abap-remote-fs has been added with HTTP transport at http://localhost:4847/mcp"*
The Complete Architecture
Claude Code can now autonomously:
- Search and read any ABAP object on the live SAP system
- write → syntax check → activate → unlock objects
- Create transport requests and assign objects
- Run ATC checks and read short dumps - all from a plain-English prompt
> Golden - In Auto mode, Claude will write, syntax check, and activate without pausing - that's the power of the agentic workflow. If you want to review before anything is activated, switch to Plan mode  shift+tab to cycle modes)  Claude will show you the full plan and wait for your approval. For production-adjacent systems, always run in Plan or Step mode.
Part 4 - Live Demo: One Prompt, One Real ABAP Program on SAP
Disclaimer: This is my first time setting up all this and explore, so i just started with a very simple scenario. Going forward will create RAP objects and other complex artifacts and see the efficiency.
Let me show you what all of this enables. Here's the exact prompt I used:
> Create a new ABAP executable program ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01 in my ZVS_DEMO package. Fetch data from MARA for a user-specified Material Number (select-option) and display Material Number, Type, and Created By in an ALV grid. Write in ABAP 7.5 syntax.
One prompt. No templates. No copy-paste. Here's what Claude did.
Act 1 - Claude Plans Before It Codes
Claude didn't immediately start writing code. It:
1. Confirmed the target package ('ZVS_DEMO') exists on the system
2. Detected that the program creation triggered a **transport request dialog** — and recognised it was the likely source of a cancellation
3. Called the SAP ADT API via Bash to **list existing open transports**
4. Selected the correct transport and retried the creation
There was a little bit delay on my end to confirm the transport. and it gave me this message.
> Package confirmed. The cancellation is likely a transport request dialog. Let me check for existing transports, then retry.
This is the part that impresses ABAP developers most. Claude handled a real SAP workflow quirk .
Act 2 - The Program Appears on SAP
Claude then created the program on the live system. You can watch its autonomous todo list mid-execution:
- Create ABAP program ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01 in ZVS_DEMO package via MCP
- Get workspace URI for the new program
- Write ABAP source code into the program
- Activate the program and check diagnostics
'ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01' appeared in the VS Code Explorer sidebar - a real, registered ABAP program on the SAP system, not a local file.
You can see initially there was no program created in my package 
After few seconds, the program is created 

 

Act 3 - Full ABAP 7.5 Source Code Written
With the object created, Claude fetched the workspace URI and wrote the complete source code directly into the SAP program. What it generated:
No placeholder code. Real, working ABAP in the correct syntax release, referencing actual MARA field types from the live system.
Act 4 - Running the Program
After activation, there are two ways to run it:
Option A - Classic SAP GUI:Log in to SAP GUI →run the program → Execute
Option B - Embedded SAP Web GUI(what I used): VS Code can open the SAP Web GUI directly in a browser tab, embedded inside the IDE. No separate login or window switching required.
Enter selection criteria:
The selection screen appeared with the Matnr  field exactly as specified. I entered a range of 11to 21. The value help (F4) shows the available materials in the system
Material Number, Material Type, and Created By - exactly what was asked for. Live data from MARA. Report running.
 
Summary:
|Claude understands SAP context| It identified the transport dialog, queried existing transports via ADT, and handled a real SAP workflow quirk autonomously 
| It created a real SAP object| 'ZCL_DEMO_REPORT_01' exists on the live system, created via ADT REST API through MCP - not a file on disk 
| Production-quality ABAP 7.5 | TYPE declarations referencing actual MARA field types, correct naming conventions, modern SQL syntax 
| Minutes, not hours| The developer's job shifts to reviewing - not writing skeletons, hunting transport dialogs, or looking up field types 
| Fully iterative| Once created, you can ask Claude to add authority checks, error handling, ALV field catalogues, or additional filters - each iteration in seconds 
| You stay in control | Claude's todo list is transparent - you see every step it plans. You review the code. You decide what activates 
 
Where to Go from Here?
Here are five things to try once you have the setup working:
1. Understand inherited code - Explain the purpose and flow of report Z_*_REPORT. Include tables accessed, authority checks, deprecated patterns like SELECT .
2. Generate a CDS view - Create a CDS view hierarchy for Sales Order analysis: interface view joining VBAK+VBAP, projection view with Fiori Elements. UI annotations for a List Report.
3. Build a Travel Request POC -  RAP Business Object with CRUD, approve/reject action, draft handling, OData V4 binding, Fiori Elements UI.
4. Write ABAP Unit tests - Generate a unit test class 
5. Code review and modernization - Review this class for Clean ABAP violations, missing authority checks, SELECT * usage, and SELECT inside LOOP. Propose S/4-equivalent replacements.
 
The bottom line: The integration that used to require manual Node.js setup, JSON editing, and external package management now takes a single Ctrl+Shift+P toggle and one terminal command.
 
The ABAP Remote Filesystem team has done the hard work. Claude Code does the coding. You do the review.
Give it a try - and let me know in the comments what use case you tackle first.
 
A personal note  : This is my first time diving deeper into vibe coding and MCP concepts. I come from a classic ABAP background, and everything covered here -  from the VS Code setup to the MCP server to the live demo - I explored and built from scratch over the past few weeks. The scenario is intentionally simple. The goal was to get the fundamentals right, document them honestly, and share what works. There is a lot more ground to cover - RAP generation at scale, Clean Core extensibility patterns, ATC pre-checks via Claude Code, and deeper agentic workflows. I'll be going further in the posts ahead. If you're also just getting started with this, I hope this gives you a solid, working foundation to build on. And if you're already further ahead - I'd love to hear what you've learnt in the comments.
 
Note: All screenshots in this post are taken from a live S/4HANA development system. Code generated by Claude Code should always be reviewed and tested in a sandbox environment before activation in any productive system.

entry.title: How to Update a Custom Field In SAP CRM Using Function Module CRM ORDER MAINTAIN
entry.content:
Introduction:


When working in SAP CRM  , a common requirement is to  programmatically update custom fields (extension fields or KUT fields) on Service orders or other Business transactions . These fields are typically added via Custom Fields and Logic  in Fiori Applications and are stored in Standard Tables . (For example :  Service Order Table CRMS4D_SERV_H)

The challenge many developers face is: how do you correctly update these fields without triggering inconsistencies, locking issues, or lost data?

In this blog post, I'll walk you through a working ABAP function module that correctly updates a custom  number field (ZZ1_CUSTOMFIELD_SRH)  on a CRM service order header — using the standard CRM order maintenance APIs.

 Lets Get Started :
The Approach: Using Standard CRM APIs
SAP CRM has its own internal framework for reading and writing order data. You should never update CRM tables like CRMS4D_SERV_H  directly via UPDATE   statements  , Doing so bypasses the CRM Business Object Layer  and Cause Data Inconsistencies

The correct sequence is:

  1. Get an internal handle using  (CRM_INTLAY_GET_HANDLE )
  2. Prepare the customer header data structure ( CRMT_CUSTOMER_H_COM)
  3. Specify which fields you are changing via the input fields table (CRMT_INPUT_FIELD_TAB )
  4. Call ( CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN )to apply changes in the CRM buffer
  5. Call ( CRM_ORDER_SAVE ) to persist the changes
  6. Commit with ( BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT ) or rollback on failure

Step-by-Step Code Walkthrough
Step 1 – Read the Existing Order Header

SELECT SINGLE * FROM crms4d_serv_h
    INTO @DATA(ls_res)
    WHERE header_guid = @iv_guid.

We first read the service order header to confirm it exists and retrieve the  header_guid for later use in the input fields structure.
Step 2 – Get a CRM Internal Handle

CALL FUNCTION 'CRM_INTLAY_GET_HANDLE'
    IMPORTING
      ev_handle = ev_ref_handle.

The handle is a unique reference used internally by the CRM framework to track your changes within the current session buffer. It must be consistent across all structures you fill.

Step 3 – Fill the Customer Header Structure

  ls_customer_h-ref_handle         = ev_ref_handle.
  ls_customer_h-ref_guid           = iv_guid.
  ls_customer_h-mode               = 'A'.
  ls_customer_h-zz1_customfield_srh = iv_custom_field.
APPEND ls_customer_h TO lt_customerh.

The  CRMT_CUSTOMER_H_COM  structure holds all custom extension fields for the service order header. Set  mode = 'A'  to update the field.

Step 4 – Define the Input Fields (What Changed)

ls_nametab = 'ZZ1_CUSTOMFIELD_SRH'.
APPEND ls_nametab TO lt_nametab.

ls_input_fields-ref_handle  = ev_ref_handle.
ls_input_fields-ref_guid    = ls_res-header_guid.
ls_input_fields-ref_kind    = 'A'.
ls_input_fields-objectname  = 'CUSTOMER_H'.
ls_input_fields-field_names = lt_nametab[].
INSERT ls_input_fields INTO TABLE et_input_fields.

This is a critical step that many developers miss. CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN uses the ct_input_fields table to know which specific fields have been changed. If you don't include a field name here, the system will ignore that field — even if you've filled it in the data structure.
ref_kind  = 'A' refers to the order header
objectname = 'CUSTOMER_H' maps to the customer header extension object

Step 5 – Call CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN

CALL FUNCTION 'CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN'
  EXPORTING
    it_customer_h   = lt_customerh
  CHANGING
    ct_input_fields = et_input_fields
  EXCEPTIONS
    error_occurred    = 1
    document_locked   = 2
    no_change_allowed = 3
    no_authority      = 4
    OTHERS            = 5.

This function writes your changes into the CRM in-memory buffer. It does not save to the database yet. Always handle the exceptions - especially (document_locked ), which means another user or process has the order open.
Step 6 – Save and Commit (or Rollback)

CALL FUNCTION 'CRM_ORDER_SAVE'
  EXPORTING
    it_objects_to_save = lt_guid
  IMPORTING
    et_saved_objects   = lt_saved_objects
  EXCEPTIONS
    document_not_saved = 1
    OTHERS             = 2.

IF sy-subrc = 0.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      wait = 'X'.
ELSE.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
ENDIF.

Always use wait = 'X' in  ( BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT ) to ensure the commit is synchronous before the calling program continues. If the save fails, roll back immediately to avoid partial data corruption.
COMPLETE FUNCTION MODULE FOR REFERENCE : 

FUNCTION CUSTOMFIELD.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IV_GUID) TYPE CRMT_OBJECT_GUID
*"     REFERENCE(IV_CONTRACT_ACC) TYPE BAPICAPARA-ACCOUNT
*"     REFERENCE(IV_PROCESS_TYPE) TYPE CRMT_PROCESS_TYPE_DB OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(IV_CUSTOMFIELD) TYPE ZZ1_CUSTOMFIELD
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  SELECT SINGLE * FROM crms4d_serv_h
    INTO @DATA(ls_res)
    WHERE header_guid = @iv_guid.

  DATA ls_input_fields TYPE crmt_input_field.
  DATA lt_nametab TYPE crmt_input_field_names_tab.
  DATA ls_nametab TYPE crmt_input_field_names.
  DATA ls_orderadm_h TYPE crmt_orderadm_h_com.
  DATA ev_ref_handle TYPE crmt_handle.

  DATA: lt_customerh    TYPE crmt_customer_h_comt,
        ls_customer_h   TYPE crmt_customer_h_com,
        et_orderadm_h   TYPE crmt_orderadm_h_comt,
        et_input_fields TYPE crmt_input_field_tab,
        lt_orderadmh    TYPE crmt_orderadm_h_comt,
        lt_guid         TYPE crmt_object_guid_tab,
        lt_saved_objects TYPE crmt_return_objects,
        ls_guid         TYPE crmt_object_guid,
        es_return       TYPE bapiret2,
        lt_orderadm_i   TYPE crmt_orderadm_i_comt,
        ls_orderadm_i   TYPE crmt_orderadm_i_com.

  " Get handle
  CALL FUNCTION 'CRM_INTLAY_GET_HANDLE'
    IMPORTING
      ev_handle = ev_ref_handle.

  CLEAR ls_orderadm_h.
  CLEAR: ls_customer_h,
         ls_nametab,
         ls_input_fields,
         lt_customerh[],
         lt_nametab[],
         et_input_fields[].

  ls_customer_h-ref_handle         = ev_ref_handle.
  ls_customer_h-ref_guid           = iv_guid.
  ls_customer_h-mode               = 'A'.
  ls_customer_h-zz1_customfield_srh = iv_customfield.

  
  ls_nametab = 'ZZ1_CUSTOMFIELD_SRH'.

  APPEND ls_customer_h TO lt_customerh.
  APPEND ls_nametab TO lt_nametab.

  CLEAR ls_input_fields.
  ls_input_fields-ref_handle  = ev_ref_handle.
  ls_input_fields-ref_guid    = ls_res-header_guid.
  ls_input_fields-ref_kind    = 'A'.
  ls_input_fields-objectname  = 'CUSTOMER_H'.
  ls_input_fields-field_names = lt_nametab[].

  INSERT ls_input_fields INTO TABLE et_input_fields.

  CALL FUNCTION 'CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN'
    EXPORTING
      it_customer_h = lt_customerh
    CHANGING
      ct_input_fields = et_input_fields
    EXCEPTIONS
      error_occurred   = 1
      document_locked = 2
      no_change_allowed = 3
      no_authority     = 4
      OTHERS          = 5.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    WRITE: sy-subrc.
  ELSE.
    CLEAR lt_guid[].
    ls_guid = iv_guid.
    APPEND ls_guid TO lt_guid.

    CALL FUNCTION 'CRM_ORDER_SAVE'
      EXPORTING
        it_objects_to_save = lt_guid
      IMPORTING
        et_saved_objects   = lt_saved_objects
      EXCEPTIONS
        document_not_saved = 1
        OTHERS             = 2.
    
     IF sy-subrc = 0.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
      EXPORTING
        wait = 'X'.
  ELSE.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
  ENDIF.

    
  ENDIF.

ENDFUNCTION.

Conclusion: 

By following this guide, you have successfully learned how to programmatically update a custom extension field in SAP CRM Web UI using the standard ABAP order maintenance framework. You created a function module that correctly uses CRM_INTLAY_GET_HANDLE, CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN, and CRM_ORDER_SAVE — ensuring data consistency without bypassing the CRM business object layer.
